title: Ultimate Venezuelan Plantain Empanada
description: Crispy plantain empanadas filled with rosemary-infused refried beans, served with a tangy glaze and a side of avocado.
instructions: Peel and slice the plantains into 1-inch thick rounds.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, shortening, and water. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth and pliable.
Roll out the dough to 1/4-inch thickness.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of refried beans in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with water and sprinkle with salt.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes until golden brown.
While the empanadas are baking, prepare the glaze by combining the sugar and water in a small saucepan.
Bring the glaze to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the empanadas from the oven and brush with the glaze.
Serve the empanadas hot with sliced avocado and a side of refried beans.
